The surgeon inserts the telescope through the left lower quadrant port and positions additional instruments through two other ports. The display port is placed nearby to visualize the working area for the right colectomy. The ileocolic vein and artery are pulled anteriorly, and dissection begins behind these vessels. The surgeon performs a precise dissection around the superior mesenteric vein (SMV) to remove lymph nodes, using a coaxial setting for ease of manipulation. The ileocolic vein is visible, and the ileocolic artery runs behind the SMV, with the vein being dissected first after clipping.

The surgeon dissects the right branch of the mesocolic vein, completing the right hemicolectomy. The patient is placed in the Trendelenburg position, and the lateral attachments are dissected.
